data.description:
## Finding

Canonical contributor guidance hardcodes a specific maintainer/bot DCO identity even though project contribution policy requires contributors to sign with their own real configured identity.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:124-169` documents the Gerrit workflow and currently prescribes `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com>`.
- `.agents/rules/rebar.md:28-34` repeats the same personal bot identity.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:198-214` requires a real contributor name and email for DCO sign-off.

## Remediation

Make the canonical guidance identity-neutral: contributors and agents use the real name and email configured for the actor performing the commit. Retain Joe Oakhart's bot identity only in narrowly scoped automation guidance for a workflow that actually runs as that bot.

Cascade flag: documentation-only contract change; verify all imported/copied agent guidance so contradictory instructions do not remain.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AGENTS.md` tells contributors and agents to verify `git config user.name` and `git config user.email` and to sign off with that configured real identity.
- [ ] `.agents/rules/rebar.md` and any derived guidance no longer instruct every actor to use Joe Oakhart's bot identity.
- [ ] Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it and is not presented as general contributor guidance.
- [ ] `CONTRIBUTING.md`, canonical agent guidance, and generated/imported guidance describe one consistent DCO contract.
- [ ] A documentation consistency test or equivalent deterministic check prevents reintroducing a globally hardcoded personal sign-off identity.
data.title: Make contributor sign-off guidance identity-neutral
